WEB_FIXTURES.title: 2026 China AI Talent Market Report
WEB_FIXTURES.content: China's AI talent gap reached approximately 1.5 million in 2026, a 20% increase over 2025. The supply-to-demand ratio for senior AI talent (5+ years of experience) is only 1:8. Median annual salary by sub-field: large language models 800K-1.5M yuan, multimodal AI 750K-1.3M yuan, embodied intelligence 850K-1.6M yuan, AI safety 700K-1.2M yuan, AIGC applications 500K-900K yuan. Embodied intelligence saw the largest salary increase at 25%. For campus recruiting, starting salaries for AI-related master's graduates from Tsinghua and Peking University exceeded 450K yuan.

WEB_FIXTURES.title: AI Talent City Distribution Atlas
WEB_FIXTURES.content: 2026 AI talent distribution by city: Beijing (32.5%), Shanghai (21.3%), Shenzhen (16.8%), Hangzhou (12.1%), Chengdu (5.7%), Nanjing (4.2%), Wuhan (3.1%), Others (4.3%). Beijing's Zhongguancun and Shanghai's Zhangjiang are the two major AI talent hubs. New trend: talent inflow to emerging first-tier cities such as Chengdu and Wuhan grew 45% year-over-year, mainly driven by local government talent attraction policies and lower cost of living. Remote work has increased the share of AI talent in second-tier cities from 8% in 2024 to 15%.

WEB_FIXTURES.title: AI Hiring Trends Insights
WEB_FIXTURES.content: 2026 AI hiring trends: 1) Demand for large model training positions decreased by 15%, while inference and deployment positions grew by 60%; 2) Demand for AI + industry cross-disciplinary talent surged, with financial AI and medical AI positions growing 80%; 3) AI safety and alignment became a new growth area, with the number of positions doubling; 4) A clear trend of foreign companies returning, with Google and Microsoft China R&D centers expanding hiring by 30%; 5) Equity incentives have become key to attracting top talent, with the average option value at Top 10 AI companies around 2 million yuan.
